title: Enemy Targeting
summary: Make the idle enemy notice and chase the nearby player while preserving the calm state outside its range.
playerOutcome: The player can approach the red enemy, see it switch from idle to chasing, and retreat beyond its range to make it stop again.
whyItMatters: This is the sample world's first visible reaction to the player, turning a static scene into a responsive gameplay encounter.
baselineBehavior: The blue player moves with the arrow keys or WASD. The red enemy is visible but always sets its velocity to zero and never detects or follows the player.
expectedBehavior: The enemy remains idle beyond a 220-pixel detection radius, reports detection within that radius, moves directly toward the player while detected, and visibly returns to idle when the player leaves the radius.

included: Direct movement toward the detected player
A plain visible idle or chasing state
Headless checks for idle, detection, chase, and preserved player movement
excluded: Animation, external art, audio, or broad enemy AI infrastructure

acceptanceCriteria.text: With the player more than 220 pixels away, 30 physics frames leave enemy.is_player_detected() false, velocity at zero, and position unchanged within 0.01 pixels.
With the player 180 pixels away, enemy.is_player_detected() becomes true within one physics frame.
While the player remains detected, 30 physics frames reduce the enemy-to-player distance by at least 10 pixels.
Arrow-key and WASD input both move the player, and simulating the existing ui_right input for 10 physics frames moves the player right by at least 1 pixel after Enemy Targeting is added.
npm run godot:verify exits successfully and emits FORGE_ENEMY_TARGETING_VERIFY_OK with idle, detection, chase, and player checks marked pass.
Launching with npm run demo:play lets a user see the enemy remain idle when far away, switch to CHASING and move when approached, then return to IDLE after the player retreats beyond the radius.

verification.instruction: Run npm run demo:play. Move with the arrow keys or WASD, approach the red enemy until its label changes from IDLE to CHASING, retreat until it returns to IDLE, then close the game.
